# Deriv Glossary

---

## API token
**Topic:** Account management
**Definition:** A unique code generated by the API provider, granting users or applications access to specific actions. It serves as an authentication method for different scopes of access such as view, trade, perform payment operation, and admin access.
**EU availability:** Available

## Account currency
**Topic:** Account management
**Definition:** The currency you choose when opening a trading account with Deriv. All trading activities, including profits, losses, deposits, and withdrawals are done in the account currency. You can select your account currency upon sign-up, which can either be in fiat or cryptocurrencies.
**EU availability:** Available

## Account limits
**Topic:** Account management
**Definition:** These are maximum amount of money or trading assets that you're allowed to trade within a specific period. These limits are set by default.
**EU availability:** Available

## Accumulate (Growth rate)
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A feature in accumulator trading. It's the rate at which you can choose to grow your stake for every tick that stays within the barrier. You can choose a growth rate between 1% and 5%.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Accumulator — Growth start step
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A feature in accumulator trading. It's the minimum number of ticks before your payout starts to grow.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Accumulator — Maximum aggregate open stake
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The maximum stake of all open trading positions opened by all traders per each growth rate. When this limit is hit, we'll freeze the opening of new positions until the total stake drops.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Accumulator Options
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A trade type that allows you to grow your potential profit exponentially as long as the price moves within the trade barriers.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Admin fee
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A type of trading account charge which applies to overnight positions in Deriv MT5 swap-free accounts after a five-day grace period.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Affiliate
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A Deriv business partner who introduces Deriv to potential new clients. Affiliates earn commissions based on the relevant plans they have subscribed to.
**EU availability:** Available

## Allow equals
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A feature in the Rise/Fall option trade type that allows you to earn a potential payout when the entry spot equals the exit spot. You'll have to enable it before opening your position.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## American Indices
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** American Indices replicate the performance of the leading publicly traded companies within a particular sector or segment of the US economy.
**EU availability:** Available

## American Options
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A derivative contract that can be exercised at any time before the expiry date. Some examples of these Options that we offer on SmartTrader are Touch/No Touch and In/Out.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Arbitrage
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A trading strategy that involves taking advantage of price discrepancies in different markets or between different instruments to earn a profit.
**EU availability:** Available

## Asian Indices
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Asian Indices replicate the performance of the leading publicly traded companies in the financial markets in Asia and Oceania.
**EU availability:** Available

## Asian Options
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A derivative contract that allows you to predict if the exit spot will be higher or lower than the average of all ticks. Some examples of these Options that we offer on SmartTrader are Asian Up and Asian Down.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Ask price
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The price a seller is willing to sell an asset to a buyer in open trades. It is the lowest price a seller is willing to accept for an asset at a given time.
**EU availability:** Available

## Asset
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Any financial instrument that holds value and can be traded such as forex, commodities, stocks, and indices, cryptocurrencies, ETFs, and derived indices.
**EU availability:** Available

## Asset class
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Also known as an asset group, it refers to a category of financial instruments. It consists of groups of similar assets that share similar characteristics and behave in the same way in a portfolio. Deriv offers a range of asset classes on our platforms, including: forex currency pairs, commodities, stocks and stock indices, cryptocurrencies, ETFs, and derived indices (consisting of basket indices, synthetic indices, and derived FX).
**EU availability:** Available

## At the money
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A term used in options trading to describe a situation where the strike of an option is equal to the current market price of the underlying asset. In other words, the option is "at the money" when there is no intrinsic value in the option — it is neither in the money nor out of the money.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Australia 200
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Also known as the ASX 200 (Australian Securities Exchange 200 Index), Australia 200 tracks the performance of the largest and most actively traded companies listed on the Australian Securities Exchange (ASX).
**EU availability:** Available

---

## Barrier offset
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** An options feature that allows you to set a target price of an underlying asset. The barrier offset represents the distance between the target and the current price.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Barrier(s)
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The target price that you set for an underlying asset on an options trade. This allows you to predict if the market price will go above, below, or remain between your price targets.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Base currency
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The first currency listed in a currency pair. It shows how much the base currency is worth as measured against the quote currency.
**EU availability:** Available

## Base rate
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The benchmark interest rate set by a central bank or monetary authority of a country. The base rate serves as the foundation for the interest rates charged by commercial banks when they lend money to customers.
**EU availability:** Available

## Basis point
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A unit of measurement used in finance to describe changes in interest rates or other financial percentages. One basis point is equal to 0.01%, or one hundredth of a percentage point.
**EU availability:** Available

## Basket indices
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Basket indices measure the value of a particular currency against a basket of five global currencies (EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, USD), each weighted by 20%. Basket trading allows you to trade your favourite currency against a basket of major currencies.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Bear
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A bear trader is someone who believes that the market or a particular asset is going to experience a decline in value. This view is known as bearish, and it typically involves selling assets that the trader owns or short selling an asset in anticipation of a market decline.
**EU availability:** Available

## Bear market
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A market condition in which prices of assets are falling, and investor confidence is low. It is generally characterised by a consistent decline in market prices, typically by 20% or more, over an extended period of time, often several months or longer. The term "bear" comes from the metaphor of a bear swiping its paws downward to attack its prey, as the downward trend of the market resembles the bear's movement.
**EU availability:** Available

## Bear market index
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A synthetic market that replicates bearish trends with constant volatility.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Bid price
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The price at which a buyer is willing to purchase an asset from a seller. The bid price in trading is the highest price a buyer is willing to pay for an asset at a given time.
**EU availability:** Available

## Bid-Ask spread
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The difference between the highest price a buyer is willing to pay (the "bid") and the lowest price a seller is willing to accept (the "ask") for a financial asset. The bid-ask spread represents the cost of trading and reflects market liquidity, with narrower spreads typically indicating more liquid markets.
**EU availability:** Available

## Bollinger Bands
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A technical analysis indicator used to assess the volatility and potential price levels of a financial instrument. It helps traders identify periods of high or low volatility, potential trend changes, and potential breakout points in the price chart.
**EU availability:** Available

## Breakeven point
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The price level at which your position's profit and loss are equal. In other words, the trade has paid for itself and there are no net profits or losses.
**EU availability:** Available

## Breakout
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Breakout in trading refers to a price movement of a trading asset beyond a significant level of support or resistance.
**EU availability:** Available

## Broker
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A person or a firm that acts as an intermediary between buyers and sellers of financial instruments. Brokers facilitate trades by connecting buyers and sellers and executing trades on behalf of their clients.
**EU availability:** Available

## Bug bounty
**Topic:** Deriv platform/features
**Definition:** Our bug bounty programme that offers rewards to individuals who identify and report bugs on our platforms. Rewards are based on the impact and severity of the reported bug.
**EU availability:** Available

## Bull
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A bull trader is someone who believes that the market or a particular asset is going to experience an increase in value. This view is known as bullish, and it typically involves buying assets that the trader believes will increase in price.
**EU availability:** Available

## Bull market
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A market condition in which prices of assets are rising or expected to rise over a prolonged period, and investor confidence is high. The term "bull" comes from the metaphor of a bull attacking its prey by thrusting its horns up into the air.
**EU availability:** Available

## Bull market index
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A synthetic market that replicates bullish trends with constant volatility.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Buy
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The act of purchasing a financial instrument with a buy order, with the expectation that its value will increase in the future. When you buy an asset, you are taking a long position in that asset.
**EU availability:** Available

## Buy limit
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A pending order to buy a trading asset at a price lower than the current market price. Buy limit orders give you control over your entry points and help prevent you from overpaying for assets. However, the fulfilment of the order depends on market conditions and the availability of sellers willing to sell at or below the specified limit price.
**EU availability:** Available

## Buy price (CFDs)
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The price at which a buyer is willing to purchase an asset. Buy price is also known as the ask price, and it is typically displayed on the right side of a quote.
**EU availability:** Available

## Buy price (Options/Multipliers)
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The amount you pay to open a trade position. With options, you won't ever lose more than this amount. Also known as a premium, it's the price you pay to purchase an options contract. Factors that influence the buy price include the asset price, strike, expiry time, market volatility, and interest rates.
**EU availability:** Available

## Buy stop
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A stop order that triggers a market buy order when the price trades at or above a specified stop price level. It is used to either close out short positions or enter new long positions if the price rises to the predefined trigger level.
**EU availability:** Available

## Buy stop limit
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A conditional order that combines a stop price and a limit price. When the market reaches the stop price, the order becomes a limit order to buy at or at better than the specified limit price, allowing you to control both the entry price and execution conditions.
**EU availability:** Available

---

## Call option
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** An option trade contract that allows you to speculate on the price of an underlying asset without owning the asset itself. You'll open a call option if you predict that the market price will rise within a specific time period.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Candlestick
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A type of chart used to display the price movements of an asset.
**EU availability:** Available

## Capital
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The amount of money that is available for trading.
**EU availability:** Available

## Central bank
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A financial institution that is responsible for managing a country's monetary policy and regulating its banking system.
**EU availability:** Available

## Closed position
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A closed position is when you complete a trade by conducting an opposite transaction, which terminates your exposure, eliminating any further potential risk or profit or loss associated with the trading asset.
**EU availability:** Available

## Closing price
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Also known as close price, this refers to the last traded price of an asset at the end of a trading session. The closing price is often used as a key parameter in technical analysis.
**EU availability:** Available

## Commission
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The fee we charge for executing a trade on our trading platforms. The amount we charge depends on the asset, market conditions, and other factors.
**EU availability:** Available

## Commodities
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A commodity is a basic raw material that can be bought, sold, and traded on exchanges around the world. When trading commodities such as metals and energy on Deriv, you'll predict the commodity prices without owning the underlying asset.
**EU availability:** Available

## Consolidation
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** This refers to a period of price movement in which the price of a financial asset remains within a defined price range, without making significant moves in either direction.
**EU availability:** Available

## Consumer price index (CPI)
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** This index is an economic indicator that evaluates inflationary trends in the market, measuring changes in the prices of goods and services over time. It is used to track market inflation and to understand the purchasing power of consumers. As the prices of goods and services increase over time, the CPI will also rise, indicating a decrease in the purchasing power of each currency unit.
**EU availability:** Available

## Continuous indices
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** These indices correspond to simulated markets with constant volatilities of 10%, 25%, 50%, 75%, and 100%. One tick is generated every two seconds for volatility indices 10, 25, 50, 75, and 100. One tick is generated every second for volatility indices 10 (1s), 25 (1s), 50 (1s), 75 (1s), and 100 (1s).
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Contract duration
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The time frame between the opening of a trade contract and its expiry. This can range from a few seconds to months or even years, depending on the trade type.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Contract for difference (CFD)
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A type of trading that allows you to speculate on the rise or fall of an underlying asset and profit from the difference between the contract's entry price and exit price without actually owning the asset.
**EU availability:** Available

## Contract size
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The standardised quantity or volume of a financial instrument that is traded in a single contract.
**EU availability:** Available

## Contract value
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The current value of a trade contract based on the initial buy price and the current profit/loss. The contract value depends on the instrument type. For CFDs, the contract value is determined by the size of the contract and the current price of the underlying asset. For options, the contract value is influenced by the strike, the current price of the underlying asset, and the number of contracts held.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Conversion rate
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The exchange rate used by a payment processor to convert funds from one currency to another, similar to currency exchange.
**EU availability:** Available

## Copy trading
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A trading strategy in which a trader can copy trades from others, typically more experienced and successful traders. You can use this feature on Deriv MT5 and Deriv cTrader to automatically replicate the trades of your favourite expert traders.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Corporate account
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** An account that is opened and maintained by a company, to manage trading accounts and activities.
**EU availability:** Available

## Crash/Boom Indices
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** These synthetic indices are designed to produce an average of one price drop (crash) or spike (boom) event in a series of 300, 500, or 1,000 ticks. A "boom" market is when the price of the underlying asset is expected to rapidly increase, while a "crash" market is when the price is expected to rapidly decrease. You can trade multipliers and CFDs on these indices.
**EU availability:** Available

## Crypto account
**Topic:** Account management
**Definition:** A trading account with cryptocurrency as the account currency. You may have multiple cryptocurrency accounts with Deriv, registered under a single email.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Crypto wallet
**Topic:** Account management
**Definition:** A digital wallet that allows traders to transfer cryptocurrencies into their Deriv crypto account and vice versa.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Cryptocurrencies
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** In trading, cryptocurrencies are digital assets that can be bought and sold on cryptocurrency exchanges. Unlike traditional currencies, they operate on decentralised, peer-to-peer networks, which means that they are not controlled by any central authority or government. Cryptocurrency trading on Deriv involves speculating on the price movements of various digital currencies, such as Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Litecoin, without owning the underlying asset.
**EU availability:** Available

## Crystallisation
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The act of converting unrealised profits or losses into actual realised profits or losses by selling the assets.
**EU availability:** Available

## Currency appreciation
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Currency appreciation is when one currency gains value in relation to another currency in the foreign exchange market. This results in the ability of one unit of the appreciating currency to purchase more units of the other currency than before.
**EU availability:** Available

## Currency depreciation
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Currency depreciation happens when a currency loses its value compared to another currency in the foreign exchange market. This means that it requires more of the depreciating currency to buy one unit of the other currency than it did before.
**EU availability:** Available

## Currency pair
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A currency pair is the quotation of two different currencies, with the value of one currency being expressed in terms of the other.
**EU availability:** Available

## Current stake
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** This refers to the current stake amount of a trade contract based on the current total profit/loss.
**EU availability:** Available

## Cut-off time
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The deadline by which a trade must be executed or submitted for processing within a specific trading day for it to be considered for that day's trading activities. The cut-off time may vary depending on the financial market traded.
**EU availability:** Available

---

## DEX Indices
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** DEX indices reflect simulated markets where asset prices react swiftly to news events, characterised by frequent small movements and occasional significant spikes or drops every 600/900/1500 seconds on average.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Daily reset indices
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A synthetic market that replicates bullish trends with constant volatility.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Day order
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A type of order in financial trading that instructs a broker to buy or sell a financial instrument at a specified price during regular trading hours on a particular trading day. The order is valid for that day only and will expire at the end of the trading day if not executed.
**EU availability:** Available

## Day trading
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A trading strategy in which traders buy and sell within the same day, with the aim of profiting from short-term price movements. Day traders don't hold any trade positions overnight, and all trades are closed before the market closes for the day.
**EU availability:** Available

## Deal
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A trading deal is a transaction that is executed on the platform when an order is filled.
**EU availability:** Available

## Deal cancellation
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A multipliers feature that allows you to cancel trade contracts within a chosen time frame without losing your stake. We charge a small fee for this. When deal cancellation is active, stop loss and take profit won't be available until the deal cancellation period expires. If the market price reaches the stop-out level before the deal cancellation period expires, we'll cancel your position and return your stake without profit or loss.
**EU availability:** Available

## Deal cancellation duration
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The time frame in which you can cancel a trade contract without losing your stake.
**EU availability:** Available

## Deal cancellation fee
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A fee we charge for the deal cancellation feature with multipliers. This fee may vary for every trade contract. You can see the fee amount when you choose deal cancellation before opening a position.
**EU availability:** Available

## Deflation
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A decrease in the general price level of goods and services over time. Opposite of inflation.
**EU availability:** Available

## Demo account
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A trading account that allows you to practise trading on Deriv using virtual funds. It is also referred to as virtual account.
**EU availability:** Available

## Deposit
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The action of transferring money from a payment account to the main trading account.
**EU availability:** Available

## Deposit limit
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The minimum and maximum deposit limits set for a specific payment method that you can use to transfer funds into your Deriv trading account. Visit our payment methods page to learn more on the different minimum and maximum deposit limits for each payment method.
**EU availability:** Available

## Deriv API
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The Deriv Application Programming Interface (API) gives you access to all trading functionalities of our Deriv Trader platform and allows you to build your own trading application using the programming language of choice. You can earn commission on trades and payments performed by your clients via the apps you create with Deriv API.
**EU availability:** Available

## Deriv Blog
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The official trading blog of Deriv, providing market insights, trading tips, educational content, and updates on the platform.
**EU availability:** Available

## Deriv Bot
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A platform where you can build your own automated trading bot using drag-and-drop "blocks", without needing any programming knowledge. Each block contains instructions that determine the bot's behaviour.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Deriv GO
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Deriv's mobile trading app that gives you access to the forex, derived indices, and cryptocurrencies market.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Deriv MT5
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A popular multi-asset CFD trading platform, developed by MetaQuotes, that gives you access to the forex, stocks & indices, cryptocurrencies, commodities, ETFs, and derived indices markets.
**EU availability:** Available

## Deriv Nakala
**Topic:** Deriv platform/features
**Definition:** Our mobile cross-platform copy trading app where you can follow and replicate the strategies of experienced traders, or share your own strategies as a provider. Visit the Deriv Nakala page to learn more about how it works.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Deriv P2P
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A peer-to-peer deposit and withdrawal service for you to buy and sell Deriv credits in exchange for your local currency. Deriv credits can be used for trading activities on our platforms. It is currently available in selected countries only. Deriv P2P is unavailable to clients residing in the EU.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Deriv Prime
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Deriv Prime is a division of the institutional trading services of Deriv Group, offering liquidity solutions and global market access through a single integration point for institutional clients.
**EU availability:** Available

## Deriv Trader
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A powerful yet easy-to-use trading platform, giving you access to the forex, commodities, cryptocurrencies, and indices market.
**EU availability:** Available

## Deriv X
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A customisable multi-asset trading platform offering CFDs on forex, commodities, exchange-traded funds, stocks & indices, cryptocurrencies, and derived indices.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Deriv cTrader
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A multi-asset trading platform offering CFDs. It has various features including copy trading, limit orders, stop orders, chart trading, and custom indicators to enrich your trading experience.
**EU availability:** Available

## Derivative
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A financial contract whose value is derived from the underlying asset or security, such as stocks, commodities, currencies, or market indices. With this type of contract, you can speculate the price movements of these financial instruments without owning them.
**EU availability:** Available

## Derived FX
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Derived FX indices are simulated assets with prices derived from the price movements of real major forex pairs. Our algorithms track real-world currency prices and dampen price fluctuations caused by news events and market sentiment. Trading derived FX offers you the opportunity to trade on simulated assets that are based on major forex pairs at the volatility level of your choice.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Derived indices
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Derived indices is a market category that consists of Deriv's in-house developed trading assets. Currently, derived markets include synthetic indices, basket indices, and derived FX. These indices have asset prices derived from real-world and simulated markets.
**EU availability:** Available

## Digital options
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A derivative trading product that allows you to predict the market price movement from 2 possible outcomes. For example, rise or fall. You'll earn a fixed payout if your prediction is correct. If the market price doesn't move in your favour, you won't lose more than your stake.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Digits — Even/Odd
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A digital options trade contract that lets you predict whether the last digit of the last tick will be an even or odd number.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Digits — Matches/Differs
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A digital options trade contract that lets you predict whether the last digit of the last tick will match a number you choose.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Digits — Over/Under
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A digital options trade contract that lets you predict whether the last digit of the contract's last tick will be higher or lower than a number you choose.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Digits options
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A trading product that allows you to speculate on the last digit of the last tick of a trade contract.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Divergence
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Divergence is a situation where the price of an asset moves in a different direction than an indicator or oscillator that is commonly used to analyse the asset's price movements.
**EU availability:** Available

## Diversification strategy
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A risk management technique that involves trading a variety of assets or securities in order to reduce the overall risk of a trading portfolio.
**EU availability:** Available

## Dividend
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A distribution of a portion of a company's profits to its shareholders. It is usually paid out in cash or additional stock shares.
**EU availability:** Available

## Dormant fee
**Topic:** Account management
**Definition:** The fee applied to Deriv trading accounts that have been inactive for a year. If the account remains inactive after the initial deduction, a fee will be charged every six months until the account is active again.
**EU availability:** Available

## Double exponential jump diffusion indices (DEX)
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The double exponential jump diffusion (DEX) indices simulate the occurrence of bullish and bearish jumps over time in reaction to positive or negative news. On average, significant jumps take place every 600, 900, or 1,500 seconds.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Downtrend
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A market condition where an asset's price is consistently decreasing over a period of time.
**EU availability:** Available

## Drawdown
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A measure of how risky an investment is by looking at how much it goes down. It shows, in percentages, how much a trading account or portfolio loses from its highest value to its lowest.
**EU availability:** Available

## Drift Switching Indices
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** One of the types of synthetic indices that simulate market trends, encompassing three regimes: positive drift (bullish trend), negative drift (bearish trend), and driftless (sideways trend).
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

---

## End time
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The time when a trade contract expires.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Energy
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Energy refers to the commodities used to generate power, such as crude oil, natural gas, gasoline, and heating oil. Deriv offers Brent crude oil and West Texas Intermediate for trading.
**EU availability:** Available

## Entry spot
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The market price when you open a position. It is the first tick at or after the start time of your trade contract.
**EU availability:** Available

## Equilibrium
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A state of balance between the supply and demand of a particular trading asset.
**EU availability:** Available

## Equities
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** In trading, equities refer to stocks that are publicly traded on a stock exchange. They represent ownership in a company and are bought and sold on exchanges like the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) or NASDAQ.
**EU availability:** Available

## Equity
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The current value of your CFD trading account in Deriv MT5. It is the sum of the account's balance and any floating profit or loss on open trades.
**EU availability:** Available

## Europe 50 (SX5E)
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The Europe 50 (also known as the EURO STOXX 50 Index) tracks the performance of the 50 largest and most liquid companies listed in 18 European countries that use the euro as their currency.
**EU availability:** Available

## European indices
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** European indices replicate the performance of the leading publicly traded companies in the financial markets across Europe.
**EU availability:** Available

## European Options
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A derivative contract that can only be exercised on its expiry date. Some examples of this trade type that we offer are Up/Down and Digits.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Exchange
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** In trading, an exchange is a marketplace where financial assets, such as stocks, indices, and currencies, are bought and sold.
**EU availability:** Available

## Exchange rate
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Exchange rate is a currency value expressed as the value of another currency.
**EU availability:** Available

## Exchange-traded fund
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Also known as ETFs, exchange-traded funds are a type of investment fund traded on a stock exchange designed to track the performance of a specific index or basket of assets, such as stocks, bonds, commodities, or currencies.
**EU availability:** Available

## Execution
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The process of completing a trade once an order to buy or sell a financial instrument has been submitted. It is carried out by a trading broker.
**EU availability:** Available

## Exhaustion
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A situation where asset price movement becomes unstable, signalling an imminent trend reversal.
**EU availability:** Available

## Exit spot
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The market price when you close a trade position. It is the last tick at or before the end time of your trade contract.
**EU availability:** Available

## Exit time
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The time at which an option contract is closed.
**EU availability:** Available

## Exotic pairs
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Forex exotic pairs are currency pairs that are not commonly traded in the forex market. These pairs typically consist of a major currency, such as US dollar, Euro, or Japanese yen, paired with a currency from a developing or emerging economy.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Expiration time
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The time at which a pending order will be cancelled if it has not been executed.
**EU availability:** Available

## Exponential moving average (EMA)
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A type of moving average that is calculated by placing greater weight and significance on recent price data, making it more responsive to recent price changes than other moving averages.
**EU availability:** Available

---

## FED
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The Fed refers to the Federal Reserve Bank, the central bank of the US, or the FOMC (Federal Open Market Committee). This is the central banking system of the US that is responsible for implementing monetary policy, supervising and regulating banks, maintaining stability in the financial system, and providing certain financial services to the U.S. government.
**EU availability:** Available

## FOMC
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) is a committee within the US Federal Reserve that implements monetary policy and influences the supply of money and credit in the economy.
**EU availability:** Available

## Fiat account
**Topic:** Account management
**Definition:** A trading account with fiat currency as the account currency.
**EU availability:** Available

## Fiat currency
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A currency issued by central banks and governments, whose value is determined by the country's economic stability. For example, the US dollar, euro, and Japanese yen.
**EU availability:** Available

## Fiat onramp
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A cashier service that enables you to add funds to your Deriv crypto account using a fiat currency payment account.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Fibonacci retracement
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A technical analysis tool used in trading to identify potential levels of support and resistance in a financial asset's price movement. It is based on the idea that after a price move, prices tend to retrace a predictable portion of that move before continuing in the original direction.
**EU availability:** Available

## Fill
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The execution of a trade order at a specified price. When an order has been completed, it is often referred to as 'filled'. There is no guarantee that every trade will become filled.
**EU availability:** Available

## Financial STP
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Financial straight-through processing (STP) means that trade orders are sent directly to liquidity providers, such as banks or other brokers, without any interference from a dealing desk. This automates the settlement processes and reduces the likelihood of errors and delays.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Financial assessment
**Topic:** Account management
**Definition:** An assessment to evaluate your financial health and performance. It involves analysing your source of income and wealth to determine your net worth and overall financial position.
**EU availability:** Available

## Financial instrument
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A type of trading asset that holds the potential to generate future cash flows or other valuable economic benefits. This can include stocks, commodities, forex, and other assets.
**EU availability:** Available

## Financial market
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A trading marketplace where buyers and sellers come together to trade financial instruments such as stocks, commodities, and derivatives.
**EU availability:** Available

## Floating exchange rate
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A type of exchange rate system where the value of a country's pricing system is determined by the supply and demand in the foreign exchange market. It is also known as flexible exchange rate.
**EU availability:** Available

## Forex
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Forex is short for foreign exchange. Forex trading, also known as currency trading or FX trading, is the process of buying and selling different currencies from around the world. It is a global decentralised marketplace where currencies are traded 24 hours a day, five days a week (Mondays to Fridays).
**EU availability:** Available

## Forward contract
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A predetermined trading contract to buy or sell an asset at a specified price and date in the future. It is also known as a trading agreement.
**EU availability:** Available

## France 40 (FCHI)
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Also known as CAC 40 (Cotation Assistée en Continu), this index tracks the performance of the 40 most actively traded companies listed on the Euronext Paris Stock Exchange.
**EU availability:** Available

## Free margin
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The amount of funds available in a trader's account that can be used to open new trading positions or absorb losses. By keeping track of your free margin, you can ensure that you have enough available funds to maintain your trades and withstand market fluctuations.
**EU availability:** Available

## Fundamental analysis
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The process of asset valuation which involves analysing the underlying macroeconomic fundamentals and financial factors of an asset. It is often used in combination with technical analysis.
**EU availability:** Available

---

## GMT
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) is the average solar time zone at the Royal Observatory in Greenwich, London. The GMT time zone is often used as a reference time for the entire world and is sometimes called Universal Time Coordinated (UTC) or Coordinated Universal Time (CUT). Deriv uses GMT all year round.
**EU availability:** Available

## GTC order
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A Good Till Cancelled (GTC) order is a trade order used in financial markets. When you place a GTC order, you are instructing your broker to keep your trade active until it is executed or until you manually cancel it.
**EU availability:** Available

## Germany 40 (GDAXI)
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Also known as the DAX (Deutscher Aktienindex), the Germany 40 index tracks the performance of the 40 largest and most actively traded companies listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange.
**EU availability:** Available

## Gross profit
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Refers to a trader's profit before factoring in any costs. This is the profit that you earn from a trade before considering costs such as fees and commissions.
**EU availability:** Available

---

## Hedging
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A trading strategy used to reduce the risk of adverse price movements by taking an offsetting position in a related asset. Hedging can help protect against potential losses, but it may also limit potential gains.
**EU availability:** Available

## High barrier offset
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A feature in the In/Out digital options trade type that allows you to set a high limit on the price of an underlying asset. The high barrier offset value depends on your trading strategy, risk appetite, and market conditions.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## High/Low ticks Options
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A digital options trade contract that allows you to predict the highest or lowest tick in a series of 5 ticks.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Hybrid Indices
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Synthetic instruments that combine the jump behaviour of Crash/Boom Indices with the structured volatility of Volatility Indices. They create a more realistic market environment by mixing predictable patterns with added volatility.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## In the money
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A situation where the current market price of the underlying asset is favourable to the options contract holder, making the option valuable for buying or selling at a more favourable price compared to the current market price.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## In/Out — Ends Between/Ends Outside
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A digital options trade contract that allows you to predict if the market price will end in or out of the trade barriers.

## In/Out — Stays Between/Goes Outside
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A digital options trade contract that allows you to predict if the market price will stay between or go out of the barriers.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## In/Out Options
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A trading product that allows you to predict if the market price will stay in or out of the barriers. You set high and low barriers and get a payout if the market price moves in your favour. Two types of In/Out trade types we offer are Ends Between/Ends Outside and Stays Between/Goes Outside.

## Inactivity fee
**Topic:** Account management
**Definition:** The fee applied to Deriv trading accounts that have been inactive for a year. If the account remains inactive after the initial deduction, a fee will be charged every six months until the account is active again.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Indicative price
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The current payout if you sell your contract before it expires. It's calculated based on the current market price and expiry time. It is useful for assessing market trends and making trading decisions. This price isn't a guarantee of your actual payout. Since it's subject to market fluctuations, the actual execution price may differ.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Indicator
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A mathematical calculation tool used to analyse the price movements of an asset and provide traders with insights into the asset's potential future price direction.
**EU availability:** Available

## Indices
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Indices, or stock indices, are a collection of stocks that represent a particular market or sector.
**EU availability:** Available

## Inflation
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A continuous rise in the overall prices of goods and services within an economy over a period of time. Inflation is measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPI).

## Instrument
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A specific financial asset or security that can be bought or sold in trading.

## Intrinsic value
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The value of an option determined by the difference between the current market price of the underlying asset and the option's strike.

## Introducing broker

**Definition:** Deriv's business partner who earns commissions from bringing in new clients to trade on Deriv's CFD trading platforms.

## Japan 225
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Also known as the Nikkei 225 index, this stock index tracks the performance of 225 large, publicly-owned companies listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

## Jump indices
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Jump indices correspond to simulated or synthetic markets with constant volatilities of 10%, 25%, 50%, 75%, and 100%. There is an equal chance of an up or down jump every 20 minutes, on average. The jump size is around 30 times the normal price volatility, on average.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Know your customer (KYC)
**Topic:** Account management
**Definition:** A process to verify the identity of clients and assess their risk levels to mitigate risk and adhere to compliance regulations. In Deriv, the KYC documents requested may include proof of identity, proof of address, and proof of wealth.
**EU availability:** Available

## Large cap
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A measure of the total value of a company's outstanding shares of stock with a market capitalisation of 10 billion USD or more. It is also referred to as "big cap".
**EU availability:** Available

## Leverage
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Leverage lets you hold larger market positions than what your initial capital would allow. This way, you would amplify both your potential profits and losses. For example, a leverage ratio of 1:1000 means that Deriv provides 1,000 USD in trading capital for every 1 USD you deposit. This gives you greater market exposure but at the same time increases your risk if trades don't go as expected. The higher the leverage ratio, the more capital you can command with your own equity deposit.
**EU availability:** Available

## Limit order
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A type of order to buy or sell a security at a specific price or better. When you place the order, it will only be executed if the asset price reaches the limit price specified.
**EU availability:** Available

## Liquidity
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Liquidity in trading refers to the degree to which an asset can be bought or sold in the market without causing a significant impact on its price. Higher liquidity indicates a more active market, and narrower spreads between bid and ask prices, allowing traders to enter or exit positions smoothly and at stable prices.
**EU availability:** Available

## Liquidity provider
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A trading institution that participates in financial markets by providing liquidity or the ability to buy or sell a particular asset, security, or currency pair.
**EU availability:** Available

## Liquidity risk
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The possibility that an asset such as a stock, bond, or other financial instrument cannot be quickly sold or converted into cash without causing a significant loss in value due to market liquidity.
**EU availability:** Available

## Long position
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A trading position where you buy instruments in the expectation that their value will increase.
**EU availability:** Available

## Lookback — Maximum multiplier
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The highest multiplier amount that you can choose to amplify your potential payout with lookback contracts.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Lookback — Minimum multiplier
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The lowest multiplier amount that you can choose to amplify your potential payout with lookback contracts.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Lookbacks — Close-Low
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A digital options contract that allows you to earn the difference between the contract's lowest and closing ticks multiplied by the multiplier of your choice.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Lookbacks — High-Close
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A digital options contract that allows you to earn the difference between the contract's highest and closing ticks multiplied by the multiplier of your choice.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Lookbacks — High-Low
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A digital options contract that allows you to earn the difference between the contract's highest and lowest ticks multiplied by the multiplier of your choice.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Lookbacks — Multiplier
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A feature in the lookbacks contract type that allows you to set a multiplier amount to amplify your potential payout. The multiplier amount is in the same currency as your trading account.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Lookbacks Options
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A trading product that allows you to earn the difference between 2 ticks multiplied by the multipliers of your choice. The lookback options we offer are: High-Close, High-Low, and Close-Low.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## Loss condition
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A situation where the value of a trade decreases, leading to a financial loss. This occurs when the price of the asset being traded moves in a direction that is unfavourable to the trade's position. It is often referred to as trade loss or loss trading.
**EU availability:** Available

## Lot size
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The amount of units or contracts of an asset that are being bought or sold in a single trade. It is used to determine the trade volume.
**EU availability:** Available

## Low barrier offset
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A feature in the In/Out contract type that allows you to set a lower limit on the price movement of the underlying asset. You set the low barrier offset based on market conditions and your risk tolerance.
**EU availability:** Not available in EU

## MT5 Zero spread account

## Major pairs
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Forex major pairs, or forex major currency pairs, are the most commonly traded currency pairs in the forex market. These pairs are made up of the currencies of the world's most developed economies, and are highly liquid with high trading volumes and narrow bid-ask spreads.
**EU availability:** Available

## Margin
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A margin is the amount of funds (expressed in the trader's account currency) required for opening and keeping a leveraged position open.
**EU availability:** Available

## Margin calculator
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A trading tool to calculate the margin required to increase your market exposure.
**EU availability:** Available

## Margin call
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A request from a broker for a trader to deposit more funds into their trading account to maintain the lowest level of margin required. When you receive a margin call, you should take immediate action to address it.
**EU availability:** Available

## Margin deposit
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The amount of funds you'd need to put in to open a leveraged trading position or to maintain the required margin for all your trading positions. The margin deposit amount for each trade is calculated based on the size of the position, leverage, and any other margin requirements.
**EU availability:** Available

## Margin requirement (%)
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The percentage of funds that you must have in your trading account in order to open or hold a position. Margin requirement is calculated as: Required margin = (volume × contract size × asset price) ÷ leverage.
**EU availability:** Available

## Market
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The platform where various financial instruments such as stocks, currencies, commodities, and derivatives are bought and sold. It is also known as a trading market or trade market.
**EU availability:** Available

## Market capitalisation
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A financial metric that measures the total market value of a publicly traded company's outstanding shares of stock, also known as market cap. The market capitalisation formula is calculated by multiplying the company's current stock price by the total number of outstanding shares.
**EU availability:** Available

## Market data
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The real-time information about market activity, such as pricing, trading volume, bid and ask, quotes, and other relevant trading statistics. You can use market data to monitor trends, assess conditions, and make informed trading decisions.
**EU availability:** Available

## Market execution
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Market execution in MT5 means executing a trade at the best available market price. You may get a different price than the one you intended if the market moves quickly.
**EU availability:** Available

## Market exposure
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The degree to which market fluctuations can affect you. It can be measured by considering various things, such as your investment size, how much the asset's price moves, and how long you intend to hold the trade.
**EU availability:** Available

## Market maker
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A financial institution or individual that offers both a buy and a sell price for a security and facilitates trading by providing liquidity to the market. Market makers play a crucial role in maintaining an orderly and liquid market.

## Market value
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The price at which a financial instrument can be bought or sold on the open market at a particular time. It represents the current asset value based on the interaction of buyers and sellers in the market.
**EU availability:** Available

## Martingale strategy
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A trading strategy that doubles the position size after each loss. While it aims to recover the previous losses, it also increases your potential losses.

**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The maximum amount of a financial instrument that you can trade in a single order.

**Definition:** The highest price target a trader can set on an option contract.

**Definition:** The maximum number of trading contracts that you're allowed to have open at any given time.

These pairs are less frequently traded than major currency pairs, and typically have wider bid-ask spreads and lower market liquidity.

**Definition:** A technical analysis tool that calculates the average price of trading assets over a particular period. It is used to identify market trends and potential support or resistance levels.

**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The highest multiplier rate that you can choose to amplify your potential profit.

## Negative balance protection
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A risk management tool offered by some brokers to protect traders from losing more than their account balance. On Deriv, if your MT5 Derived account balance becomes negative due to stop out, your account balance will be automatically brought back to 0.00 in your account currency. Negative balance protection is designed to cover trading losses and does not provide protection against other charges or fees.

## Pip
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Pip, or percentage in point, is a standard unit used to measure how much the asset value changes. When an asset has a quote with five or three decimal places, we use a smaller unit called a 'pipette'. A pipette is one-tenth the size of a pip. Pipettes help measure smaller price movements more accurately.
**EU availability:** Available

## Pip calculator
**Topic:** Deriv platform/features
**Definition:** A trading tool to determine the pip value of a trade. Knowing the value of each pip in a trade is important because it can help you understand the possible risks and rewards involved.

## Pip value
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** Refers to how much 1 pip is worth, which is the smallest value change in a currency's exchange rate.

**Topic:** Deriv platform/features
**Definition:** The profit and loss margin calculator, or PnL margin calculator, is a trading tool to estimate the stop loss and/or take profit levels as well as the pip value. This calculator can help you determine the potential profit or loss on the trade.

## Portfolio
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A collection of trading assets that are held by a trader.

## Position
**Topic:** Trading focus

## Position trading
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A trading strategy in which positions are held for weeks to months or even years for long term investment.

**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The period of trading that occurs after the regular market has closed for the day. It is also known as after-hours trading.

**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** An estimate of the profit or loss based on the current market price of the underlying asset and the current value of the options contract. It is not realised until you choose to exercise or sell the trade contract before expiry time.

**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The period of trading that occurs before the regular market opens for the day.

## Profit
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** The amount you earn after deducting any associated costs from your trades. It is the difference between an asset's buy and sell price or a trade's opening and closing price.

## Profit factor
**Topic:** Trading focus
**Definition:** A financial metric that compares the total profits generated by successful trades to the total cost incurred by losing trades.
**EU availability:** Available

**Topic:** Account management
**Definition:** A type of document used to confirm a person's current country of residence. A proof of address can include a variety of documents, such as a utility bill or bank statement.

## Proof of identity (POI)
